question: Who actually pays income tax in India?

theme.label: Economy and public money
readerQuestions: What is the headline economic number?
Is this a total number or a per-person number?
How much of the story is prices, exchange rates, or GDP share?
What does this explain about household life?

visualPlan.title: Share of income-tax returns that owe zero tax

visualPlan.whyThisMatters: This is the chart that answers the page question most directly.

plannedCharts.title: Most filers owe nothing
plannedCharts.subtitle: CBDT Income Tax Return Statistics · assessment year 2012-13 to 2023-24
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The single most surprising fact: filing a return and paying tax are very different things.

plannedCharts.title: The income you can earn before paying any tax
plannedCharts.subtitle: Section 87A rebate + basic exemption history · by assessment year · general individual, most favourable regime
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The zero-tax majority is engineered by policy: Parliament keeps lifting the income at which tax becomes nil.

plannedCharts.title: Returns filed vs returns that pay
plannedCharts.subtitle: CBDT ITR Statistics · assessment year
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The true taxpaying base is the lower line — about 3 crore, not the headline ~8 crore filers.

plannedCharts.title: 0.1% of filers pay most of the income tax
plannedCharts.subtitle: CBDT ITR Statistics · AY2023-24 · how every rupee of income tax splits
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Income tax in India rests on a needle point: a tiny group at the top pays most of it.

plannedCharts.title: Individuals now out-pay companies
plannedCharts.subtitle: CBDT Time-Series · financial year · ₹ crore
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The quiet structural shift: the personal income tax has overtaken corporate tax.

plannedCharts.title: How individuals' income is earned
plannedCharts.subtitle: CBDT ITR Statistics · individual taxpayers · income by source · assessment year
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: For individual taxpayers, where does the declared income actually come from?

plannedCharts.title: The system tilted toward direct taxes
plannedCharts.subtitle: CBDT Time-Series · direct as % of central taxes · financial year
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: Direct taxes (on income) are more progressive than indirect taxes (on spending).

plannedCharts.title: Among the cheapest tax systems to run
plannedCharts.subtitle: CBDT Time-Series · cost of collection · financial year
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: How much it costs the state to collect ₹100 of direct tax.

plannedCharts.title: India's tax-to-GDP is mid-low
plannedCharts.subtitle: UNU-WIDER / ICTD · general government, incl. social contributions · latest year
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: How much of the economy the state collects in tax, versus peers.

plannedCharts.title: Personal income tax: a narrow base
plannedCharts.subtitle: OECD Revenue Statistics; India from CBDT · personal income tax · % of GDP
plannedCharts.whyThisChart: The sharpest cross-country cut: how much personal income tax each economy raises.

selectionRules: Start from the page question, not from the indicator name.
Choose the smallest set of visuals that explains scale, trend, structure, comparison, and caveat.
Prefer mobile-friendly charts: line charts with sparse ticks, horizontal bars, share strips, pyramids only when essential, and no dense tables.
Use one strong pull quote only when the evidence supports it with a number.
Add glossary blocks for technical terms that normal readers may not know.
caveats: Use only figures present in lockedNumbers or sourceSummaries.
If a related theme indicator is present, you may use it for context, but do not pretend it is the main answer.
If source coverage is thin, say so directly.
Do not compare states, districts, or cities unless the evidence packet contains those geographies.
forbiddenClaims: Do not infer causality from a trend unless the evidence explicitly supports it.
Do not cite a source that is not present in sourceSummaries.
